stories.title: **Headline:** Murakami's 12 Home Runs Lead MLB — Historic Start for Japanese-Born Slugger

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami has 12 home runs in 29 MLB games.

He leads the majors.
He's hit 454 feet.
He tied the White Sox franchise record for consecutive games with a HR.

The most surprising slugger in baseball right now is playing on the South Side.

#WhiteSox #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Murakami's first 12 MLB extra-base hits have ALL been home runs.

That's a record dating back to at least 1900 — surpassing Dae-ho Lee's 10 in 2016.

A Japanese slugger is leading MLB in home runs. And it's not even close.

#WhiteSox #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Yordan Alvarez Leads MLB in 11 Offensive Categories — On Pace for Historic Season

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Yordan Alvarez leads the AL in 11 different offensive categories.

Hits. HR. RBIs. BA. OBP. SLG. OPS. OPS+. Total bases. rOBA. IBBs.

ALL 11.

The Astros are 11-18.

He is carrying a roster while putting together one of the best offensive Aprils in recent memory.

#Astros #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Since 2022, the OPS+ leaders in MLB:

1. Aaron Judge — 206
2. Yordan Alvarez — 174
3. Shohei Ohtani — 173

Alvarez is in the company of the two greatest hitters of this generation.

And he's on pace for 68 home runs this year.

#Astros #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** NL Central Is Only MLB Division with All 5 Teams Over .500 — Historic Situation

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Every single NL Central team is over .500.

That has happened this late in a season exactly once since baseball went to divisions in 1969.

Only 3 of these 5 teams make the playoffs.

The most brutal race in baseball is happening RIGHT NOW.

#Cubs #Cardinals #Reds #Brewers #MLB

article.headline: Every NL Central Team Is Winning — And If It Stays That Way, It Would Be an MLB First

stories.title: **Headline:** April in Baseball — Braves and Dodgers MLB-Best 20-9, Yankees Right Behind at 19-10

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
April is over. Here's where things stand:

🔥 Braves: 20-9 (MLB-best, first to 20 wins)
🔥 Dodgers: 20-9 (tied ML-best)
🔥 Yankees: 19-10 (AL East leaders)

❄️ Mets: 9-19
❄️ Phillies: 9-19
❄️ Astros: 11-18 (despite Alvarez)

May baseball starts now. #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Aaron Judge Has 12 Home Runs — Chasing Career 400 with Historic First-Inning Output

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge has 5 of his first 9 home runs in the FIRST INNING.

In first-inning at-bats this season: .450 BA, 1.726 OPS.

He's at 380+ career HRs and the 400 milestone is on the horizon.

Don't overlook the best hitter in the AL. #Yankees #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** Ohtani's April Masterclass — 0.38 ERA Pitching, .996 OPS Hitting, 46-Game On-Base Streak

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Shohei Ohtani's April by the numbers:

⚾ Pitching: 0.38 ERA — leads all MLB qualified starters
🏏 Hitting: .996 OPS, 5 HR
📊 On-base streak: 46 games (longest active in MLB)

The Dodgers are 20-9.

He's playing a different sport. #Dodgers #MLB

stories.title: **Headline:** April Players to Watch in May — The Stars Setting the Tone for 2026

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
5 names to watch as May begins:

1. Murakami — 12 HR, MLB lead
2. Alvarez — 11 HR, .347 BA, 11 categories led
3. Judge — 12 HR, chasing career 400
4. Ohtani — 0.38 ERA + .996 OPS
5. Henderson — 9 HR, career-high barrel rate

Buckle up. #MLB
